Title: Subtelomeric assembly of a multi-gene pathway for antimicrobial defense compounds in cereals

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates β-amyrin at the C-23 position to form 23-hydroxy-β-amyrin.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial/antifungal root defense compound that protects against soil-borne diseases such as take-all
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces the missing C-23 hydroxylation step required for avenacin A-1 biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS; purification of product; NMR structural elucidation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in root tips in Avena strigosa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter with CPMV-HT 5′UTR and CaMV35S terminator in transient expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, Hi-C, optical map and RNA-seq in ENA PRJEB25739; RNA-seq sample ERS2620539
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: bAS1/Sad1; CYP51H10/Sad2; CYP72A475/Sad6; CYP72A476; AAT1; UGT91G16; TG1/Sad3; MT1/Sad9; UGT74H5/Sad10; SCPL1/Sad7
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; leaves harvested 5 days after infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for complete pathway engineering of avenacin production in cereals together with the other cluster genes for disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of two previously uncharacterized co-expressed CYP genes in the cluster; validated as one of the last two missing steps.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP94D65 is a co-expressed cluster P450 in Avena strigosa root tips. 2. Transient co-expression with bAS1/Sad1 in Nicotiana benthamiana produced 23-hydroxy-β-amyrin. 3. LC-MS and NMR confirmed CYP94D65 catalyzes C-23 hydroxylation, filling a missing avenacin pathway step.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces the aldehyde group at C-30 of the avenacin scaffold, and this oxidation requires prior glycosylation at C-3.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms late pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial/antifungal root defense compound that protects against soil-borne diseases such as take-all
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes the previously missing C-30 oxidation step required for avenacin A-1 formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with pathway enzymes; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in root tips in Avena strigosa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter with CPMV-HT 5′UTR and CaMV35S terminator in transient expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, Hi-C, optical map and RNA-seq in ENA PRJEB25739; RNA-seq sample ERS2620539
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: bAS1/Sad1; CYP51H10/Sad2; CYP72A475/Sad6; CYP94D65; AAT1; UGT91G16; TG1/Sad3; MT1/Sad9; UGT74H5/Sad10; SCPL1/Sad7
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; leaves harvested 5 days after infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required component for full avenacin pathway reconstruction in heterologous hosts and cereal engineering for fungal disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Earlier tests against early intermediates showed no activity; this study demonstrated that activity depends on prior C-3 glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP72A476 is a co-expressed cluster P450 in Avena strigosa. 2. It catalyzes C-30 oxidation to install the aldehyde group in the avenacin pathway. 3. Its activity depends on prior C-3 glycosylation, explaining earlier failure to detect activity on early intermediates.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to the triterpene scaffold β-amyrin.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms core scaffold of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial/antifungal root defense compound protecting against soil-borne pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the initial triterpene scaffold required for all downstream avenacin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior mutant cloning/characterization referenced in this paper; pathway reconstitution by transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in root tips in Avena strigosa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter with CPMV-HT 5′UTR and CaMV35S terminator in transient expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, RNA-seq in ENA PRJEB25739
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of early steps does not cause obvious detrimental growth effects according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential anchor gene for assembling the full avenacin pathway in cereal engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located closest to the telomere among pathway genes; used as cytogenetic marker of the cluster.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bAS1/Sad1 encodes the first committed step of avenacin biosynthesis. 2. It is part of the subtelomeric avenacin cluster and is root tip-expressed. 3. Inclusion of bAS1/Sad1 is required for full pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an early oxidation/modification step of the β-amyrin scaffold in avenacin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms early pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial/antifungal root defense compound protecting against soil-borne pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required early pathway enzyme in the 12-gene avenacin cluster and full pathway reconstitution.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior characterization referenced in paper; inclusion in transient full-pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in root tips in Avena strigosa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter with CPMV-HT 5′UTR and CaMV35S terminator in transient expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, RNA-seq in ENA PRJEB25739
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of early steps does not cause obvious detrimental growth effects according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core pathway gene to include in avenacin engineering stacks.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located close to bAS1/Sad1 near the telomere, consistent with pathway colinearity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP51H10/Sad2 is an early avenacin pathway P450 encoded within the subtelomeric cluster. 2. It is preferentially expressed in root tips. 3. It is one of the enzymes required for complete heterologous pathway reconstruction.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a previously characterized oxidation step in avenacin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial/antifungal root defense compound protecting against soil-borne pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required oxidation enzyme in the avenacin biosynthetic pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior characterization referenced in paper; inclusion in transient full-pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in root tips in Avena strigosa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter with CPMV-HT 5′UTR and CaMV35S terminator in transient expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, RNA-seq in ENA PRJEB25739
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary member of multi-gene avenacin engineering packages.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of the physically clustered and root tip co-expressed avenacin genes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP72A475/Sad6 is a characterized avenacin pathway P450 within the cluster. 2. It is root tip-expressed and genetically/physically linked to the other pathway genes. 3. It was included in successful reconstitution of the full avenacin pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Previously characterized avenacin pathway enzyme required before terminal pathway completion.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms late pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial/antifungal root defense compound protecting against soil-borne pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for avenacin biosynthesis; defects in AAT1 affect downstream C-30 oxidation dependency through altered C-3 glycosylation context.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior characterization referenced in paper; inference from mutant accumulation patterns; inclusion in full pathway transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in root tips in Avena strigosa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter with CPMV-HT 5′UTR and CaMV35S terminator in transient expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, RNA-seq in ENA PRJEB25739
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful component of full avenacin engineering pathway; proper glycosylation context is important for downstream oxidation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutants unable to perform glycosylation at C-3 were cited as lacking C-30 aldehyde-containing intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT1 is part of the complete 12-gene avenacin cluster. 2. Mutant evidence linked AAT1-dependent C-3 glycosylation context to subsequent C-30 oxidation. 3. It was required in the reconstructed pathway that produced avenacin A-1.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds one of the terminal sugars to the avenacin trisaccharide chain.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial/antifungal root defense compound protecting against soil-borne pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required late glycosylation step; mutants accumulate toxic intermediates and show severe root stunting.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior mutant analysis referenced in paper; full pathway transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter with CPMV-HT 5′UTR and CaMV35S terminator in transient expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, RNA-seq in ENA PRJEB25739
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes toxic intermediate accumulation and severe root stunting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Must be included with downstream detoxifying/finishing steps when engineering avenacin to avoid accumulation of phytotoxic intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Positioned furthest from the telomere together with TG1/Sad3, potentially reducing self-poisoning risk after telomeric deletions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT91G16 encodes a late glycosyltransferase in avenacin trisaccharide assembly. 2. Mutations cause toxic intermediate accumulation and severe root stunting. 3. Its late position and dependence relationships are important for safe pathway engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds one of the last two sugars to the avenacin trisaccharide chain through transglucosylation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial/antifungal root defense compound protecting against soil-borne pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required late glycosylation step; mutants accumulate toxic intermediates and display severe root stunting.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior mutant analysis referenced in paper; DNA FISH localization; inclusion in full pathway transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter with CPMV-HT 5′UTR and CaMV35S terminator in transient expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, RNA-seq in ENA PRJEB25739
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes toxic intermediate accumulation and severe root stunting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical finishing gene that should be stacked with upstream genes to avoid phytotoxic intermediate build-up in engineering applications.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used with Sad1 in FISH to show physical proximity within the subtelomeric cluster; positioned furthest from telomere among pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TG1/Sad3 is a late transglucosidase in avenacin trisaccharide assembly. 2. Mutations lead to toxic intermediate accumulation and severe root stunting. 3. It is physically close to Sad1 in the subtelomeric cluster and essential for complete avenacin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a methylation tailoring step in avenacin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms late pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial/antifungal root defense compound protecting against soil-borne pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required tailoring enzyme in the avenacin pathway and included in successful full pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior characterization referenced in paper; inclusion in full pathway transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in root tips in Avena strigosa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter with CPMV-HT 5′UTR and CaMV35S terminator in transient expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, RNA-seq in ENA PRJEB25739
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Part of the complete avenacin engineering module.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MT1/Sad9 is a late tailoring methyltransferase in avenacin biosynthesis. 2. It is part of the root tip-expressed subtelomeric cluster. 3. It was included in the set of genes that enabled avenacin A-1 production in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a glycosylation step in avenacin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial/antifungal root defense compound protecting against soil-borne pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required glycosyltransferase in the avenacin pathway and full pathway reconstitution.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior characterization referenced in paper; inclusion in full pathway transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in root tips in Avena strigosa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter with CPMV-HT 5′UTR and CaMV35S terminator in transient expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, RNA-seq in ENA PRJEB25739
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required member of full avenacin engineering stacks.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Related gene UGT74H7 in the cluster has low activity toward avenacin acyl precursors and was not part of the core pathway output.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74H5/Sad10 is a characterized avenacin glycosyltransferase in the cluster. 2. It is root tip-expressed and co-clustered with other pathway genes. 3. It contributed to complete heterologous synthesis of avenacin A-1.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a late acylation-related tailoring step in avenacin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for terminal modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial/antifungal root defense compound protecting against soil-borne pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required late tailoring enzyme for complete avenacin A-1 production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior characterization referenced in paper; inclusion in full pathway transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in root tips in Avena strigosa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter with TMV Omega UTR and Sad7 terminator in transient expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, RNA-seq in ENA PRJEB25739
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required for full-end product assembly in avenacin metabolic engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used a distinct TMV Omega UTR/Sad7 terminator construct in transient expression setup.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCPL1/Sad7 is a late tailoring enzyme in avenacin biosynthesis. 2. It is part of the subtelomeric, root tip-expressed gene cluster. 3. Inclusion of SCPL1/Sad7 was necessary for full avenacin A-1 reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana.
